pressure_scenarios: Domain health ruined by blast campaigns
contradictions: Personalization industry vs relevancy-first
operational_adaptations: Track win rate not just meetings
ai_era_implications: AI outbound without insight increases noise

intelligence.source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Procurement shuts down if too novel… you have to educate legal, risk, success metrics before tech.
intelligence.core_belief: Novel AI purchases fail in legal/procurement unless process education is budgeted and paid.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Simple language; clearly different from incumbents; parallel service contract to teach HITL, evals, risk framework.
contradictions: Move fast AI vs enterprise change management
operational_adaptations: Time-box services with explicit tech migration triggers
ai_era_implications: Compliance and evals belong in first SOW not v2 product

intelligence.pattern_name: Sell services to educate the budget
intelligence.source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: 40–50% have to sell some form of service before technology… paid to educate.
intelligence.core_belief: When buyers lack process for novel problem, services revenue is GTM not failure.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Co-author scope; sell education/process design; time-box; parallel tech plus service contracts.
hidden_assumptions: Services budget larger than innovation budget
pressure_scenarios: 18-month wait if you don't own education
failure_modes: Competitor educates account while you wait
contradictions: SaaS metrics vs services wedge
behavioral_signals: Buyers ask HITL and metrics questions
operational_adaptations: Truncate services; onboard users before tech gate
ai_era_implications: PMs spec HITL and evals in services SOW—they become product requirements

intelligence.pattern_name: Sell the alpha not the feature
intelligence.source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Sell the alpha, not the feature… they're buying into where you're going and the problem you're solving.
intelligence.core_belief: Enterprise buyers at $1M–$10M ARR fund asymmetric insight, not feature parity.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Lead with counter-narrative and problem thesis; defer feature catalog until buyer confirms pain is strategic.
contradictions: Product maturity needed eventually despite alpha sell
behavioral_signals: Buyer asks for vision not RFP checklist
emotional_political_dynamics: Buyer fear of career risk on novel vendor
operational_adaptations: Vision narrative tied to buyer's measured pain
ai_era_implications: AI alpha is workflow transformation not model benchmark

intelligence.source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: When you're vulnerable… not fully built yet… easier to tell someone before you make this mistake.
intelligence.core_belief: Buyers protect founders' feelings when they think product is done; honesty requires plausible incompleteness.
intelligence.operational_pattern: First call: state early stage, passion for problem, request how problem manifests—no demo-first; book second call on first call.
contradictions: April Dunford educate-market later vs Jen learn-first early
emotional_political_dynamics: Founder fear of no problem here
operational_adaptations: Gate: is problem measured? How tried to solve? Book next meeting live
ai_era_implications: AI demos dazzle while hiding workflow fit—delay demo until problem validated

intelligence.source_episode: "Sell the alpha, not the feature": The enterprise sales playbook for $1M to $10M ARR | Jen Abel
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Low conversion is okay if win rate is high… more at-bats vs better messaging.
intelligence.core_belief: Meetings without win rate signal are vanity; high win rate with low volume is positioning problem not effort problem.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Track win rate by segment; if win rate high, increase targeted at-bats; if low, fix insight/ICP before scaling outbound.
hidden_assumptions: Enough opportunities to measure win rate
contradictions: Volume culture vs Jen's craft outbound
ai_era_implications: AI can write emails but not counter-narrative—that stays founder-owned

intelligence.source_episode: Jenny Wen
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Hire block-shaped designers—80th percentile in multiple skills—not narrow T specialists.
intelligence.core_belief: Design role stretching PM/eng-ward requires multi-domain depth not single craft.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Recruit for strong generalists across research, visual, systems, prototyping; expect role flex.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Design team hiring in blurred-role AI org
hidden_assumptions: Block-shaped rare and hard to hire
Depth may suffer in all domains
contradictions: Block hiring vs need for deep accessibility/expertise
behavioral_signals: Block vs T framework in hiring
ai_era_implications: Block-shaped designers best suited for AI-blurred roles

intelligence.pattern_name: Design forced to change by engineering velocity
intelligence.source_episode: Jenny Wen
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Engineering changed more than design so far—but eng speed forces design role change regardless.
intelligence.core_belief: Design transformation is downstream of eng transformation in AI companies.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Anticipate design role compression on upfront phases; expand integrator/vision roles.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Design function in AI product company
hidden_assumptions: Design could lead not just follow
failure_modes: Copying old process in new speed
contradictions: Design as follower vs strategic leader
ai_era_implications: Eng velocity from AI is forcing function for design ops change

intelligence.pattern_name: Design split: execution support vs 3-6mo vision
intelligence.source_episode: Jenny Wen
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Two modes: help engineers ship cohesively now; create 3-6 month direction via prototype not 10-year deck.
intelligence.core_belief: Design must operate on two horizons with different artifacts.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Staff execution-support designers on active ships; protect capacity for horizon vision prototypes.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Head of Design in fast-shipping AI org
hidden_assumptions: Small teams struggle to split roles
Vision work deprioritized under ship pressure
contradictions: 3-6mo vision vs need for long-term brand
ai_era_implications: Vision horizon shrinks as capability shifts accelerate

intelligence.pattern_name: Don't Trust the Process—design process is dead
intelligence.source_episode: Jenny Wen
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Trust the process design gospel is basically dead—research diverge/converge cycles too slow for AI-era engineering.
intelligence.core_belief: Preserved design process becomes liability when engineering ships continuously.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Replace phase-gate design with embedded support + short-horizon vision; prototype over deck.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Design org in AI-native product company
hidden_assumptions: Some domains still need rigorous research
failure_modes: Big upfront research missing real constraints
contradictions: Process skepticism vs quality/accessibility needs
ai_era_implications: Process compression permanent as eng velocity rises

intelligence.source_episode: Jenny Wen
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Legibility framework helps make prototypes communicate intent clearly to eng and stakeholders.
intelligence.core_belief: Fast prototypes fail when intent isn't legible to collaborators.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Apply legibility framework to prototypes so eng can extend without misreading design intent.
contradictions: Legibility investment vs speed
ai_era_implications: AI-generated prototypes need legibility discipline more not less

intelligence.pattern_name: Let engineers cook—don't block rapid shipping
intelligence.source_episode: Jenny Wen
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Better off not blocking engineers shipping rapidly—let them cook; design supports and connects.
intelligence.core_belief: Blocking eng velocity destroys learning speed; design adds value post-ship integration.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Support in-flight shipping with cohesion passes; guide rather than gate early experiments.
contradictions: Let them cook vs brand/cohesion needs
ai_era_implications: Eng-led ship default increases design integrator role

intelligence.source_episode: Jenny Wen
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Launch Cowork as research preview—worst it'll ever be; put out because internal belief in power despite flaws.
intelligence.core_belief: Labeled early shipping builds trust through honesty and iteration speed.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Brand launches as research preview with explicit caveats; design supports known flaws while capturing learning.
failure_modes: Preview used to excuse quality neglect
contradictions: Preview honesty vs user frustration
ai_era_implications: Preview cadence requires design for iteration not polish

intelligence.pattern_name: Too much surface area for one designer
intelligence.source_episode: Jenny Wen
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Not no designer—too much for one designer when eng ships across many surfaces simultaneously.
intelligence.core_belief: Design bottleneck shifts from approval to coverage.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Prioritize high-blast-radius surfaces; accept lighter touch on research previews; hire block generalists.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Small design team vs many product surfaces
contradictions: Minimal design involvement vs quality
ai_era_implications: AI multiplies surfaces designers must cover

intelligence.pattern_name: Ask for more time when needed
intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: People afraid to ask for more exec time miss critical insight—counterpoint to busy calendar reality.
intelligence.core_belief: Insufficient time guarantees failed influence; asking for time is professional not annoying.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Request dedicated working sessions for complex bets; don't cram strategic pitches into leftover slots.
contradictions: Ask for time vs respect busy calendar
operational_adaptations: Pre-brief docs to reduce time needed
ai_era_implications: Async AI prep reduces but doesn't replace live time

intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Executive calendars are like a strobe light—8am already urgent; finance, interviews, people problems, legal, product review in sequence.
intelligence.core_belief: Exec attention is fragmented bursts; your problem is rarely centered unless you align to their moment.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Map exec attention rhythms; batch asks; pre-align to current top-of-mind bets; never assume open calendar means availability for your agenda.
hidden_assumptions: Some execs protect deep work blocks
failure_modes: Assuming exec saw your async doc
contradictions: Strobe-light model vs exec accessibility rhetoric
operational_adaptations: Ask what's top of mind before pitching
ai_era_implications: AI can simulate exec pushback but not replace live alignment

intelligence.pattern_name: Failed buy-in is your fault not theirs
intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: If leaders didn't buy into your idea, you couldn't influence them—you can't blame them for agreeing to something else.
intelligence.core_belief: Influence failure is owned by the pitcher not the exec.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Retrospect on misfit framing/timing; iterate influence approach; don't externalize rejection.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: PM/product leader accountable for stakeholder alignment
contradictions: Accountability frame vs psychological safety for ICs
ai_era_implications: AI simulation reduces but doesn't eliminate live rejection

intelligence.pattern_name: Hour-turnaround framework response to exec invitation
intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Rachel mentioned design reviews; Kev had loom framework within an hour—high-risk vs low-risk changes, blast radius.
intelligence.core_belief: Exec subtle invitations are tests of organizational alignment speed.
intelligence.operational_pattern: When exec raises concern, deliver framework/proposal within hours unprompted; slot into org incentive to move fast.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Design leader in democratized shipping org
hidden_assumptions: Not all exec comments require immediate response
contradictions: Hour response vs sustainable workload
behavioral_signals: Loom frameworks within hour of exec comment
ai_era_implications: AI enables hour-turnaround frameworks at scale

intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Politics is manipulating for your own gain; influence is increasing odds your good ideas survive.
intelligence.core_belief: Influence requires empathy for stakeholder incentives, not manipulation.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Frame proposals in exec incentive language; center their goals not your ego; treat failed buy-in as your influence failure.
hidden_assumptions: Politics still exists in resource allocation
Good ideas can still lose to politics
contradictions: Anti-politics framing while operating in political org
ai_era_implications: AI helps prep pitches but human trust still gates decisions

intelligence.pattern_name: Kill projects to build exec trust
intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: One of the biggest things you can do to build trust is kill things, deprioritize things—shows aligned incentives not ego.
intelligence.core_belief: Willingness to kill own work signals senior company-first thinking execs trust.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Proactively deprioritize/kill projects misaligned with exec bets; frame kills as aligned incentives not failure.
failure_modes: Never killing anything—seen as not senior
contradictions: Kill culture vs innovation need
ai_era_implications: AI makes starting easy—increases value of visible killing

intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: That's so interesting—what led you to believe that? Opens influence conversations without defensiveness.
intelligence.core_belief: Curiosity disarms exec disagreement better than argument.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Lead with genuine learning questions in influence conversations; avoid premature defense of your idea.
contradictions: Learning mindset vs need to drive closure
ai_era_implications: AI can suggest curiosity prompts for objections

intelligence.pattern_name: Pick up subtle exec feedback cues
intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Fourth time Tamara asked for top 10 use cases—she got frustrated we'd not documented it; people miss deep cues.
intelligence.core_belief: Repeated exec questions signal unresolved strategic anxiety, not nagging.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Treat repeated asks as P0 documentation/decision work; respond within hours with frameworks not acknowledgments.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Product leadership team in strategy reviews
hidden_assumptions: Repeats may be forgetfulness not urgency
contradictions: Fast response culture vs thoughtful depth
ai_era_implications: AI can flag repeated themes in meeting notes

intelligence.pattern_name: Simulate exec with AI before live pitch
intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Train GPT on product review transcripts so PMs run PRDs through 'what will Rachel push back on?'
intelligence.core_belief: Exec pushback patterns are learnable and simulatable.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Build exec-specific AI simulators from past reviews; stress-test pitches for predictable objections before live meetings.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Product org with documented review culture
hidden_assumptions: Transcripts may not represent current priorities
failure_modes: Over-reliance on simulator vs live learning
contradictions: AI simulation vs human relationship building
ai_era_implications: Influence prep becomes primary AI PM use case

intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: As skills democratize—everyone ships code/design/PRDs—strategy clarity becomes the binding constraint.
intelligence.core_belief: When execution is cheap, unclear strategy creates faster chaos not faster progress.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Invest leadership time in strategy clarity and review gates for high-blast-radius work; reduce PRD bureaucracy for low-risk ships.
contradictions: Strategy clarity advocacy vs fast shipping culture
behavioral_signals: Exec asking for documented use cases
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear that strategy slows AI-enabled speed
operational_adaptations: Strategy docs for top bets only
ai_era_implications: Democratized building makes strategy the scarce leadership input

intelligence.source_episode: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can't replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Give three options—not one—so execs see you considered alternatives; middle option often wins.
intelligence.core_belief: Optionality signals rigor and guides decisions without appearing naive.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Present recommended option among three considered paths; use packaging psychology deliberately.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Product leader in exec review culture
hidden_assumptions: Can feel manipulative if options fake
contradictions: Three-option tactic vs genuine recommendation
emotional_political_dynamics: Exec impatience with long option menus
ai_era_implications: AI can generate option sets faster—increases need for genuine tradeoff analysis

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Duolingo balance: big swings on new bets while optimizing core—both required; either alone stalls.
intelligence.core_belief: Exploration and exploitation are concurrent jobs not phases.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Portfolio budget: percent resources on swings; percent on CURR optimization; review quarterly.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Leadership team with explicit swing list and owner.
hidden_assumptions: That optimization is boring so skip it; that swings excuse core neglect.
pressure_scenarios: Investor wants new product line; core decaying.
failure_modes: Swing fails; core weakened; no floor.
contradictions: Turnaround story includes both discipline types.
behavioral_signals: All swings no ops; or all ops no innovation.
operational_adaptations: Swing postmortems; core regression tests on CURR.
ai_era_implications: AI enables more swings—without optimization discipline companies die faster.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: CURR—current user retention rate—became north star aligning product, marketing, and monetization on kept users not touched users.
intelligence.core_belief: One metric reduces religious wars if chosen correctly.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Define CURR precisely; report weekly; tie experiments to CURR delta not vanity.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Duolingo-style org with single hierarchical metric.
hidden_assumptions: That DAU alone drives stock story; that metrics are apolitical.
pressure_scenarios: Exec wants MAU for narrative; team optimizes wrong loop.
failure_modes: Submetric gaming; short-term CURR hurt by monetization push.
contradictions: North star purity vs multiple competing goals at scale.
behavioral_signals: Teams claim CURR win on definitional change.
operational_adaptations: Lock definition; audit changes; pair with learning outcome metric.
ai_era_implications: AI products need CURR analog—session return not token count.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: CURR impact on DAU was six times its impact on MAU—optimizing the wrong headline metric misallocates years of team effort.
intelligence.core_belief: North star choice changes which projects win; DAU and MAU levers are not interchangeable.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Report lever rankings per metric; align team to one primary growth metric per phase; defer MAU vectors until DAU model saturated.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Executive debate on investor narrative vs operational focus.
hidden_assumptions: That improving DAU automatically satisfies MAU story.
pressure_scenarios: IPO pressure for MAU; board asks for both.
failure_modes: Wrong team rewarded; MAU stalls while DAU soars.
contradictions: Focused DAU for years knowing MAU needs later bets—explicit tradeoff.
behavioral_signals: Sensitivity tables in growth model reviews.
operational_adaptations: Publish which metric each experiment targets before ship.
ai_era_implications: AI analytics make multi-metric modeling easier—choosing one primary metric still required.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Personalized difficulty and content path drove retention—one-size-fits-all lesson is operational malpractice.
intelligence.core_belief: Learning products must adapt faster than user boredom.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Invest in adaptation engine; A/B path algorithms; measure learning not just time-on-app.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Data science embedded in curriculum squad.
hidden_assumptions: That content library size equals quality; that teachers scale linearly.
pressure_scenarios: Model costs; cold start for new languages.
failure_modes: Generic path; bored advanced users; overwhelmed beginners quit.
contradictions: Human teachers vs AI adaptation—both needed at scale.
operational_adaptations: Expose adaptation visibly so users trust system.
ai_era_implications: LLM tutoring makes adaptation table stakes—static courses die.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Social competition works among strangers with similar levels—not friends only—because embarrassment dynamics differ.
intelligence.core_belief: Social design must match relationship graph reality.
intelligence.operational_pattern: League strangers not friend leaderboard; reset cycles to reduce demotivation.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Game designers tuning league segmentation weekly.
hidden_assumptions: That Facebook friend graph is required for virality.
contradictions: Stranger leagues success vs schools banning competitive ranking.
behavioral_signals: Users cheat leagues; bots in leaderboard.
emotional_political_dynamics: Parents vs growth on child competition.
ai_era_implications: AI opponents in leagues—new fairness debates emerge.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Gardenscapes moves counter took months, changed nothing on retention, and the team disbanded—neutral results are worse than failure because they consume morale.
intelligence.core_belief: Borrowed mechanics without strategic fit waste quarters and teach the wrong lesson if you do not postmortem context.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Kill neutral experiments fast; run three-question borrow framework before build; document why similarity masked difference.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Gamification team copying mobile games after exec buy-in.
hidden_assumptions: That visual similarity to hit games implies mechanic transfer.
pressure_scenarios: Pressure to show gamification progress after DAU stall.
failure_modes: Team dissolution drama; abandoned idea without learning captured.
contradictions: Retention-first thesis coexists with months spent on wrong mechanic.
behavioral_signals: Expectant launch monitoring; silence on metrics.
emotional_political_dynamics: Deflation and blame after neutral read.
operational_adaptations: Mandatory adaptation memo before any borrowed mechanic ships.
ai_era_implications: AI makes copying mechanics faster—neutral tests multiply without discipline.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Notifications are a scarce trust channel—over-notification trains ignore and kills retention lever.
intelligence.core_belief: Every push is a withdrawal from a finite trust account.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Cap frequency; personalize timing; measure uninstall and mute not just open rate.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Growth team with notification budget per user per week.
hidden_assumptions: That more reminders equals more learning.
failure_modes: Notification blindness; brand annoyance; OS-level mute.
contradictions: Streak mechanics depend on notifications—tension with protection.
operational_adaptations: Model optimal send time; kill campaigns that spike unsubscribes.
ai_era_implications: AI personalized nudges can restore trust or destroy faster.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Uber-style referral gave free Super month but best active users already had premium—strategy needed advocates it structurally excluded.
intelligence.core_belief: Referral economics must reward the people who will actually refer, not hypothetical users.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Map advocate identity before choosing reward; subscription products need different playbook than pay-as-you-go rides.
hidden_assumptions: That viral playbooks transfer across business models.
failure_modes: Plateau; wasted iterations; best users ignored.
contradictions: Uber inspiration opening vs Duolingo failure—same article.
operational_adaptations: Reward status or currency power users lack—not redundant subscription month.
ai_era_implications: AI could personalize referral rewards—still requires advocate fit analysis.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Duolingo reignited growth by fixing retention before buying traffic—acquisition on a leaky bucket is vanity.
intelligence.core_belief: Retention is the honestest growth metric.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Pause paid scale until core loop retention improves; reallocate to product quality and habit.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Growth team subordinate to learning outcomes metric.
hidden_assumptions: That CAC solves everything; that viral marketing fixes bad product.
pressure_scenarios: Board pressure for user count; competitor ad spend.
failure_modes: Burn on ads; churn hides; unit economics collapse.
contradictions: Classic growth vs Mazal retention-first turnaround story.
operational_adaptations: Hold acquisition spend gate on CURR or retention threshold.
ai_era_implications: AI personalization raises retention bar—acquisition without it wastes more.

intelligence.source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Streaks compound habit—but also create anxiety and brittle motivation—operational balance required.
intelligence.core_belief: Loss aversion drives return visits until it drives shame quit.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Streak freeze; repair mechanics; pair streak with real learning minutes.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Live ops team monitoring streak-induced churn cohorts.
hidden_assumptions: That infinite streak growth is always good.
failure_modes: Burnout users leave permanently; negative press on dark patterns.
contradictions: Streak hero story vs mental health criticism.
behavioral_signals: Users cheat timezone; support tickets explode.
ai_era_implications: AI coaches may replace dumb streaks with competence streaks.

intelligence.pattern_name: AI increases importance of taste and judgment
intelligence.source_episode: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: When code is abundant, judgment about what to build becomes the scarce resource.
intelligence.core_belief: Building is cheap; selecting problems is expensive.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Elevate founders/operators with taste; reduce PM layers that only coordinate.
hidden_assumptions: Taste is hard to hire for
contradictions: Anti-research vs need for user harm understanding
ai_era_implications: Judgment/taste become primary moats

intelligence.source_episode: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Don't overemphasize failures or people won't raise hands for hard problems.
intelligence.core_belief: Failure tolerance must be asymmetric—punish only avoidable misses, celebrate bold tries.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Skip blame retros; focus forward on winning; critique missing foresight not ambition.
hidden_assumptions: Public critique can chill risk if tone wrong
contradictions: Anti-retro vs need for postmortems in infra
ai_era_implications: AI failures cheaper—more shots on goal if culture supports

intelligence.source_episode: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: High performance machines don't have psychological safety. They're about winning.
intelligence.core_belief: Winning cultures tolerate public critique and ambitious failure over comfort.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Criticize in public; skip failure retros that chill risk-taking; reward bold attempts.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Competitive startup with public accountability norms
hidden_assumptions: Psychological safety may still aid learning in some contexts
contradictions: Anti-retros vs learning from mistakes
ai_era_implications: AI doesn't remove need for human accountability norms

intelligence.pattern_name: Barrel definition: outcome ownership hell or high water
intelligence.source_episode: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Can they take an idea and make it happen? Get us over that hill come hell or high water.
intelligence.core_belief: True operators accumulate resources, motivate, measure, and deliver outcomes—not tasks.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Promote and entrust by expanding outcome scope after narrow wins; don't confuse domain expertise with barrelness.
hidden_assumptions: Narrow early wins may not generalize
contradictions: Outcome ownership vs psychological safety critique culture
ai_era_implications: Agents assist barrels but accountability stays human

intelligence.source_episode: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: PayPal had 254 people but only 12-17 barrels; hiring more people doesn't increase parallel important work.
intelligence.core_belief: Organization throughput is barrel-limited not headcount-limited.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Identify barrels early via outcome ownership; assign ammunition deliberately; avoid hiring that increases coordination tax without new barrels.
hidden_assumptions: Barrel scope can grow with trust
contradictions: Anti-headcount growth while investing in many companies
behavioral_signals: Intern solves smoothie problem on day two
ai_era_implications: AI ammunition multiplies barrel output but doesn't create barrels

intelligence.source_episode: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Enterprise needs customer research; consumer often doesn't—different playbooks.
intelligence.core_belief: B2B and B2C product discovery are incompatible operational models.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Apply research-heavy GTM in enterprise; vision+tempo in consumer within same portfolio only with explicit separation.
contradictions: Simplifies bifurcation—many hybrids exist
behavioral_signals: Different advice to portfolio companies by segment
emotional_political_dynamics: PM identity tied to research methods
ai_era_implications: AI accelerates both playbooks differently

intelligence.pattern_name: Criticize in public for collective problem-solving
intelligence.source_episode: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Critique in public so everyone learns the play being executed.
intelligence.core_belief: Private feedback hides organizational learning.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Default to public critique with factual tone; reserve private for trust/personal issues.
contradictions: Public critique vs psychological safety debate
ai_era_implications: AI transcripts make public critique more permanent—tone matters more

intelligence.pattern_name: No customer research for consumer products
intelligence.source_episode: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: For consumer, talking to customers is often misleading; vision and iteration matter more.
intelligence.core_belief: Consumer users can't articulate breakthrough products; founders must have taste.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Ship vision-driven prototypes; iterate from usage data not interviews; reserve research for enterprise.
failure_modes: Building wrong vision without any signal
contradictions: Directly opposes Evan's deep listening approach
ai_era_implications: Faster iteration reduces need for research but increases ship-wrong risk

intelligence.source_episode: State of the product job market in 2025

intelligence.core_belief: Geography matters less for job access, more for career compound interest.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Invest in remote-native visibility: writing, demos, async leadership proof.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Distributed team with intentional in-person summits not daily office mandate.
hidden_assumptions: That remote equals equal opportunity; that async needs no skill.
pressure_scenarios: Company mandates return-to-office for leadership track.
failure_modes: Invisible PM loses promo to office neighbor; timezone penalty.
contradictions: Celebrates remote openness while Bay Area gravity section admits concentration.
operational_adaptations: Document decisions in public channels; fly to key summits quarterly.
ai_era_implications: Remote PMs need agent-augmented visibility—bots do not replace narrative presence.

intelligence.source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Generate docs from the codebase after the fact instead of writing specs that instantly lie—truth flows from what runs.
intelligence.core_belief: Living code is the only honest spec in agentic development.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Ship feature, then agent writes user-facing and internal docs from diff, then human edits for intent.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Team where technical writers pair with agents on release branches.
hidden_assumptions: That upfront PRDs stay accurate; that agents capture why not only what.
failure_modes: Docs describe deprecated paths; missing rationale for tradeoffs.
contradictions: Advocates reverse docs while schools still teach PRD-first waterfall.
behavioral_signals: Docs lag releases by quarters; support answers from stale help center.
emotional_political_dynamics: Writers fear obsolescence; PMs fear loss of narrative control.
operational_adaptations: Doc PRs required before merge to main; link doc commit to feature flag.
ai_era_implications: Documentation becomes continuous export from repo—static wikis die.

intelligence.source_episode: An AI glossary
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: RLHF teaches models to please raters—which can diverge from truth, safety, or your product values.
intelligence.core_belief: Helpful-sounding is not the same as correct or on-brand.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Test for sycophancy and refusals; add product-specific evals beyond vendor defaults.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Trust and safety reviewing model upgrades for new failure modes.
hidden_assumptions: That base model alignment equals your alignment.
pressure_scenarios: Launch in regulated domain; model refuses valid tasks or lies politely.
failure_modes: Brand damage from overly agreeable assistant; user manipulation via flattery.
contradictions: Explains RLHF limits while vendors imply safety solved.
behavioral_signals: Users report creepy agreeableness; PM blames prompt.
emotional_political_dynamics: Ethics vs growth on personality tuning.
operational_adaptations: Custom rubric judges; A/B on tone with retention and truth metrics.
ai_era_implications: Every product needs RLHF-on-RLHF—layered alignment for brand.

intelligence.source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Use agents to compare what leadership said in meetings and docs versus what shipped in git—close the loop like a COO with perfect memory.
intelligence.core_belief: Organizations drift when narrative and execution are not continuously reconciled.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Ingest transcripts plus commits; weekly diff report of promises vs merges.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Chief-of-staff function partially automated for scale-ups.
hidden_assumptions: That leaders intend follow-through when they forget; that public shaming is the goal.
pressure_scenarios: All-hands commitments; OKR season; reorg rhetoric.
failure_modes: Weaponized reports; culture of surveillance; false positives from ambiguous language.
contradictions: Empowers IC truth-telling while risking political backlash.
behavioral_signals: Leaders stop speaking precisely; Slack goes oral-only.
emotional_political_dynamics: Trust collapse between exec and product org.
operational_adaptations: Private team-level reports first; human adjudication before escalation.
ai_era_implications: Every company gets an AI COO—winners use it for learning not punishment.

intelligence.source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Docs that update themselves when code changes—treat documentation as a CI job, not a quarterly project.
intelligence.core_belief: If docs are not coupled to release mechanics, they are fiction.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Hook agent on merge to main: summarize diff, update affected pages, open PR for human skim.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Platform eng owning doc-bot with PM review on customer-facing pages.
hidden_assumptions: That teams will manually update; that auto-docs are good enough without edit.
pressure_scenarios: Major refactor; rename explosion; API breaking changes.
failure_modes: Nonsense auto-generated pages; SEO spam internal wiki.
contradictions: Automation promise vs need for human voice on sensitive topics.
behavioral_signals: Support tickets cite wrong docs; customers trust less.
emotional_political_dynamics: Tech writers vs eng on blame for wrong doc.
operational_adaptations: Human-in-the-loop approval for external docs; auto-only for internal.
ai_era_implications: Docs become test artifacts—if doc PR fails CI, release blocks.

intelligence.source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: The strongest filter for vibe-coded products is daily self-use—if you do not use it, it is a demo not a product.
intelligence.core_belief: Dogfooding is the only honest eval when build is free.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Mandate seven-day self-use before sharing; kill if you reach for the old tool.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Builder who deletes more projects than they publish.
hidden_assumptions: That launch day usage predicts retention; that friends' praise counts.
pressure_scenarios: Social media pressure to announce; hackathon deadlines.
failure_modes: Ghost tools; broken auth you never hit; embarrassed silence after launch.
contradictions: Survey of what people use vs what they tweet—contradiction by design.
behavioral_signals: Product Hunt spike; author never opens app again.
operational_adaptations: Public build log with use metrics; shame OK for killed tools.
ai_era_implications: Self-use telemetry becomes default analytics for solo builders.

intelligence.source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Your repository is the go-to-market surface—README, examples, and MCP configs sell the product better than landing pages alone.
intelligence.core_belief: Developers buy what they can clone and run in thirty seconds.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Ship eval fixtures, sample apps, and integration folders as first-class product; measure clone-to-activation.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Dev-tool company where docs live beside code and releases are tagged in git.
hidden_assumptions: That marketing site traffic matters more than GitHub stars for PLG.
pressure_scenarios: Competitor fork; open-source alternative; enterprise wants on-prem.
failure_modes: Stale examples; broken quickstart; brilliant product with empty examples folder.
contradictions: Preaches repo-as-GTM in newsletter economy still obsessed with webinars.
behavioral_signals: Sales promises features not in repo; eng maintains examples as afterthought.
emotional_political_dynamics: Tension between devrel and product on who owns samples.
operational_adaptations: CI fails if examples break; treat sample drift as P0 bug.
ai_era_implications: AI agents read your repo to sell for you—bad structure equals invisible product.

intelligence.source_episode: An AI glossary
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Models are pretrained, then fine-tuned, then RLHF'd, then tool-connected—each layer adds capability and new failure modes.
intelligence.core_belief: No single knob controls behavior; stack literacy prevents magical thinking.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Map your product bet to which layer actually needs to change before retraining fantasies.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: ML platform team educating PMs on what fine-tune vs prompt vs RAG solves.
hidden_assumptions: That bigger base model makes fine-tune unnecessary; that RLHF fixes all safety.
pressure_scenarios: Vendor promises one-click custom model; compliance asks for explainability.
failure_modes: Wrong layer investment; months lost fine-tuning when prompt plus eval would suffice.
contradictions: Glossary simplifies stack while vendors obscure for sales.
behavioral_signals: PMs say we need fine-tune without data; eng sighs.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of looking dumb in ML meetings.
operational_adaptations: Decision tree poster in wiki; escalate to platform when layer unclear.
ai_era_implications: PMs must know ladder—budget and timeline conversations depend on it.

intelligence.source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: When execution is cheap, taste—not tooling—becomes the scarce differentiator between slop and products people love.
intelligence.core_belief: More builders does not mean more good products.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Invest in reference sets, design critique, and kill criteria before scaling AI output.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Small senior circle with veto on customer-facing quality.
hidden_assumptions: That volume of experiments discovers taste automatically.
pressure_scenarios: Competitor ships AI feature parity; board wants AI everywhere.
failure_modes: Feature zoo; brand dilution; users churn from inconsistent quality.
contradictions: Essential reading list elevates taste while ecosystem rewards shipping slop.
behavioral_signals: Metrics up on engagement hacks; NPS flat; design complaints dismissed.
emotional_political_dynamics: Design vs growth fight; PM caught optimizing clicks.
operational_adaptations: Taste rubric in PR review; fewer bigger bets with editorial bar.
ai_era_implications: Taste becomes hiring moat—tasteless orgs drown in AI-generated sameness.

intelligence.source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: The largest adoption blocker for Claude Code is terminal phobia—products must meet people in GUI or they forfeit half the org.
intelligence.core_belief: Interface shape determines who gets to be a builder.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Offer GUI wrappers, VS Code extensions, and guided flows before demanding shell fluency.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Company with parallel tracks: terminal-native power users and GUI-first PMs.
hidden_assumptions: That everyone will learn bash if the tool is good enough; that CLI is the only real path.
pressure_scenarios: Designers and PMs asked to just use the terminal; IT blocks shells.
failure_modes: Two-class organization: builders vs beggars; shadow no-code tools proliferate.
contradictions: Celebrates terminal power while acknowledging most people will never live there.
behavioral_signals: PMs ask eng for one-line changes; expensive pairing sessions.
emotional_political_dynamics: Shame in workshops; seniors gatekeep real workflow.
operational_adaptations: Invest in desktop apps and IDE plugins; terminal optional not mandatory.
ai_era_implications: Agent UIs replace terminals for 80%—CLI remains power-user minority.

intelligence.source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: The builders who win combine curiosity, agency, and communication—missing one collapses the triangle under AI load.
intelligence.core_belief: Skills are necessary but attributes determine whether skills compound.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Hire and coach for all three; do not promote brilliant curmudgeons or articulate passengers.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: High-trust team with explicit feedback on communication gaps.
hidden_assumptions: That coding skill alone suffices; that communication is soft optional.
pressure_scenarios: Remote async; cross-functional AI projects; exec visibility scarce.
failure_modes: Brilliant builder nobody follows; great communicator no delivery.
contradictions: Timeless attributes advice in era obsessed with tool tips.
behavioral_signals: Silent high performers overlooked; loud mediocre promoted.
emotional_political_dynamics: Identity tied to being the smart one vs being effective.
operational_adaptations: 360 on communication; pairing curious juniors with agency mentors.
ai_era_implications: AI rewards communicators who can steer agents—curiosity without communication wastes tokens.

intelligence.source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Vibe coders report consolidating stacks because tool sprawl has higher cognitive cost than feature gaps.
intelligence.core_belief: Fewer tools with deeper fluency beats infinite beta signups.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Pick one IDE, one agent, one deploy path for a quarter; measure throughput not novelty.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Small team standardizing on Cursor plus one backend.
hidden_assumptions: That best-of-breed always wins; that FOMO misses nothing important.
pressure_scenarios: New model drop weekly; influencer stack posts daily.
failure_modes: Migration whiplash; lost context; no muscle memory.
contradictions: Newsletter ecosystem profits from tool churn while advising consolidation.
behavioral_signals: Calendar full of trying new apps; roadmap empty.
operational_adaptations: Tool budget: one experiment slot per month; document why switched.
ai_era_implications: Consolidation winners capture agents—second-tool markets shrink.

intelligence.source_episode: An AI glossary
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Vibe coding means building by feel with AI—but the term now spans hobbyists, pros, and marketing slop.
intelligence.core_belief: Shared language matters because hiring, policy, and quality bars depend on it.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Define vibe coding level in your org: exploratory, prototype, or production; different gates for each.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Company with vibe coding sandbox separate from production SDLC.
hidden_assumptions: That everyone means the same thing when they say vibe code.
pressure_scenarios: Exec mandates vibe code everything; security panic.
failure_modes: Production incidents from hobby workflow; talent mismatch in hiring.
contradictions: Glossary defines term while headline culture dilutes it.
behavioral_signals: Job posts say vibe code; interview expects leetcode.
emotional_political_dynamics: Identity fights between real engineers and new builders.
operational_adaptations: Written policy on what vibe coding can touch; prod requires review.
ai_era_implications: Term splits—professional agentic engineering vs casual vibe coding.

intelligence.source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Survey highlights women building in public at rising rates—visibility does not equal funding or enterprise adoption yet.
intelligence.core_belief: Democratized tools lower build barrier; institutions lag on capital and trust.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Amplify builders' work with credit; design communities that reduce harassment tax of public building.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Diverse indie builders network sharing stacks and safety practices.
hidden_assumptions: That build access alone fixes representation in outcomes.
pressure_scenarios: Harassment in replies; dismissive investors; caregiving time constraints.
failure_modes: Talented builders go private; lose network effects of public learning.
contradictions: Celebrates progress while data still shows funding gaps.
emotional_political_dynamics: Exhaustion from being symbol and builder at once.
operational_adaptations: Paid communities; sponsorship; intentional spotlight without tokenism.
ai_era_implications: Lower build barrier shifts fight to distribution and capital—new bottlenecks.

intelligence.source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Huge premium on people able to take responsibility... in order to lead you must learn to obey.
intelligence.core_belief: Agency matters but structure and discipline enable it.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Build initiative in kids/ICs; pair with grounding rules; avoid pure rule-following culture.
contradictions: Live player language
California agency discourse

intelligence.source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Job of programmer was calculators in rooms... now argue with coding bots.
intelligence.core_belief: Each abstraction layer redefines programmer tasks not eliminates humans.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Learn to evaluate agent output; orchestrate parallel bots; depth still matters for debug.
pressure_scenarios: Scripting wasn't real programming fight repeats
contradictions: Seniors monitoring ten terminals
operational_adaptations: Teach kids code reading while agents write
ai_era_implications: Historical pattern says humans stay in loop at new abstraction

intelligence.source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: If we didn't have AI we'd panic about depopulation... remaining human workers at a premium.
intelligence.core_belief: Labor scarcity and low productivity growth context reframes AI as necessity.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Plan for human premium skills; don't assume endless labor supply; pair AI with population trends.
contradictions: Optimistic labor scarcity takes
ai_era_implications: Human relationship work gains relative price

intelligence.source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: AI transfers sand into thought—the philosopher stone.
intelligence.core_belief: Fundamental commodity shift is intelligence on tap.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Teach kids to leverage AI as lever; spend spare hours training with models.
contradictions: 10-year-old arguing with AI for fun
ai_era_implications: Bloom 2-sigma via LLM at scale

intelligence.source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Mexican standoff between PM, designer, coder—each believes AI lets them do the other two jobs.
intelligence.core_belief: All three roles are partially right; combination beats silo.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Encourage cross-skilling with AI; reward triple-threat builders; don't protect stovepipes.
contradictions: Coders shipping designed prototypes
PMs running agents
ai_era_implications: Super-empowered individuals outproduce three-person pods

intelligence.source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Productivity growth last 50 years very low... AI hits economy that had almost no technological progress.
intelligence.core_belief: Felt digital change ≠ measured economic acceleration until now.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Under-price one-dimensional 'AI eats world'; model interaction with demographics and institutions.
contradictions: Bits progress vs atoms stagnation debate
operational_adaptations: Target sectors with verifiable answers first
ai_era_implications: Reasoning/medical domains with evals see gains first

intelligence.source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Really great people becoming spectacularly great... not twice as good, ten times.
intelligence.core_belief: AI widens performance distribution at individual level.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Invest in top talent agency; give leverage tools; expect wider output spread.
contradictions: 10x coder anecdotes
Friends reporting step-change
operational_adaptations: Homeschool plus AI tutoring for depth
ai_era_implications: Winners are spectacularly great hybrids; hire for agency plus craft

intelligence.pattern_name: Task Loss Not Job Loss Frame
intelligence.source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Everybody wants to talk about job loss, but really look at task loss. The job persists longer than individual tasks.
intelligence.core_belief: Work redesign unit is tasks within jobs not job titles.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Map task bundles for PM/eng/design; swap tasks via AI; expect titles to lag task reality.
hidden_assumptions: AI replaces companies in one wave
contradictions: Executives now do own email
ai_era_implications: Orchestrating agents is new task layer on engineering

intelligence.source_episode: Matt MacInnis — 10 contrarian leadership truths (Rippling)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Deliberately understaff every project—overstaffing creates politics and work on priorities 6-15.
intelligence.core_belief: Unknown optimal staffing → bias understaff vs overstaff.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Default lean teams on top 5 priorities only → add resources surgically when dehydrated → avoid ambiguous backlog consumption.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Rippling high-growth with constant resource asks.
hidden_assumptions: That visible busyness equals progress; that headcount equals commitment.
pressure_scenarios: Exec asks for more headcount; competitor ships faster rumor.
failure_modes: Under-under-staff critical path; burnout if winning streak ends.
contradictions: Demands extraordinary effort while understaffing—tension by design.
behavioral_signals: 20-person project on item 12 on stack rank.
emotional_political_dynamics: Managers hire to grow empire (Parkinson/Stewart echo).
operational_adaptations: Pair with escalation culture and PQL quality bar.
ai_era_implications: AI leverage rewards understaffed teams with high-agency people, punishes overloaded bureaucracies.

intelligence.source_episode: Matt MacInnis — 10 contrarian leadership truths (Rippling)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Best executives tossed into disarray functions—MacInnis avoided R&D until forced; COO→CPO to fix product dumpster fire.
intelligence.core_belief: Generalist fixers must eventually tackle core product/engine, not only G&A/sales.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Rotate firefighting to worst function → first-principles rebuild → minimal process where low beta needed (PQL).
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Rippling R&D smoke: architecture, adoption measurement, leadership hiring mistakes.
hidden_assumptions: COO success means product is fine; external criticism from sidelines is enough.
pressure_scenarios: Dual hat eng/product leader; hypergrowth smoke without customer impact yet.
failure_modes: Perennial adjacent fixes while core R&D rots; over-process or under-process wrong arenas.
contradictions: Anti-process rhetoric but implements PQL rigid bar.
behavioral_signals: CEO payroll dogfood (Parker runs 5200-person payroll); ship to Parker gate.
operational_adaptations: Idiosyncratic tools (PQL) with teeth; dogfood rituals.
ai_era_implications: Product-engine chaos from AI shipping velocity needs injured-bird leaders earlier.

intelligence.source_episode: Matt MacInnis — 10 contrarian leadership truths (Rippling)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Founder CEO has purest ambition; each management ring can be order-of-magnitude intensity drop—dangerous.
intelligence.core_belief: Organizations thermodynamically cool without active preservation of founder energy.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Leaders escalate; give direct feedback (withholding is selfish); never optimize local comfort over company outcomes.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Rippling/Parker CEO intensity as renewable resource to ring-drain.
hidden_assumptions: That professionalism equals comfort; that consensus is virtue.
pressure_scenarios: Middle management layer thickening; remote/hybrid dilution.
failure_modes: Teams optimize for local comfort; feedback hoarding; Friday night triage avoided.
contradictions: Extraordinary effort necessary but not sufficient.
behavioral_signals: Comfortable employees after big win; low escalation volume.
emotional_political_dynamics: Manager fear of being disliked vs founder bluntness.
operational_adaptations: Friday escalations as separator of great vs good teams when winning.
ai_era_implications: AI agents don't preserve intensity—human leaders must still model urgency.

intelligence.source_episode: Matt MacInnis — 10 contrarian leadership truths (Rippling)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Parker: you don't really learn from mistakes—you learn from successes; join winning teams.
intelligence.core_belief: Success patterns are higher fidelity than failure rationalizations.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Resume heuristic: candidate at company during explosive growth → interview; Inkling 9yr vs Rippling 7yr learning delta cited.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Airbnb/Brian never-relax gas pedal; Apple death march.
hidden_assumptions: Failure porn builds character equally; luck not acknowledged.
pressure_scenarios: Candidates from failed startups only; moral pressure to 'never quit.'
failure_modes: Survivorship bias in hiring; staying in losing game for lessons.
contradictions: Also discusses Inkling failure lessons with respect.
emotional_political_dynamics: VC 'never quit' vs founder quit rationality.
operational_adaptations: Chase winning environments when learning is goal.
ai_era_implications: Rapid winners (Lovable) concentrate learnable AI-native patterns; losers replicate outdated playbooks.

intelligence.pattern_name: Column B vision with chaos-to-clarity ladder
intelligence.source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Column B: dream the magical future… ladder to the moon with rungs… chaos to clarity: write it down, pitch deck, designs, prototype.
intelligence.core_belief: Breakthroughs require improbable future backcast into embarrassing first steps.
intelligence.operational_pattern: 10-year vision → mission pillars → crazy big goals → incremental clarity steps per project.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Mission-driven product company at global scale
pressure_scenarios: Daily ops pull to Column A
contradictions: Crazy big goals vs reliable delivery dates
behavioral_signals: No vision deck for new initiatives
ai_era_implications: AI features must ladder to Column B mission not hype bolt-ons

intelligence.pattern_name: Crazy big goals as motivation device
intelligence.source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Crazy Big Goals… feel inadequate before them… that's motivating not demoralizing.
intelligence.core_belief: Goals should embarrass you slightly or they aren't directing enough energy.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Set public CBGs with celebration at milestones; pair with rungs so inadequacy converts to action.
contradictions: CBG ambition vs investor quarterly cadence
operational_adaptations: CBGs at company level; OKRs at execution level
ai_era_implications: AI compresses rungs—CBGs may need shorter horizons

intelligence.source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Empower the world to design… design shouldn't be gatekept by experts with expensive tools.
intelligence.core_belief: Long-horizon mission justifies multi-year infra bets that don't optimize quarterly metrics.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Align roadmap to democratization thesis; measure success by non-designer output quality not only ARR.
contradictions: Democratization vs pro/designer segment revenue
behavioral_signals: Pro tier exists but mission stays broad
operational_adaptations: AI features framed as empowerment not replacement
ai_era_implications: AI lowers design skill floor—mission validated but moat shifts to distribution and brand

intelligence.pattern_name: Operational chaos to clarity on every bet
intelligence.source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Chaos to clarity on every project… write it down, pitch deck, designs, prototype—don't keep it in your head.
intelligence.core_belief: Unwritten ideas create alignment illusions; clarity artifacts scale beyond founder intuition.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Mandatory vision deck per initiative before resourcing; prototype as alignment not only sales.
contradictions: Clarity process vs move fast
operational_adaptations: Lighter deck for small bets; full for CBG-level
ai_era_implications: AI can generate decks faster—human must still own Column B thesis

intelligence.source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: We pitched 100+ times… find investors who get the vision, not lean startup purists for Canva.
intelligence.core_belief: Wrong investor tribe wastes years; right tribe funds unconventional paths.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Iterate deck for objections but don't change Column B vision for wrong audience; seek design-believers.
contradictions: Persist vs pivot debate
behavioral_signals: Same vision different deck over years
Investor meetings cluster by thesis fit
operational_adaptations: Track investor archetype not only yes/no
ai_era_implications: AI companies face same tribe problem—technical vs GTM investors

intelligence.source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: 100+ investors said no… add slide on market size, competitive gap, problem-first pages.
intelligence.core_belief: Investor rejection is structured feedback if converted into deck mutations.
