contradictions: Persist vs find new tribe
behavioral_signals: Pitch starts with solution not problem
emotional_political_dynamics: Frustration vs investors wrong but helpful
ai_era_implications: AI pitch decks need problem/society slides not model features

intelligence.source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Front-end rewrite estimated six months, took two years—no product shipped… product company not shipping is not fun.
intelligence.core_belief: Platform rewrites are multi-year morale bets; shipping identity must be gamified to survive.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Bath-toy board game standups; accept CBG slippage while protecting platform foundations.
contradictions: Ship weekly vs platform pause
operational_adaptations: Only enter tunnel with clear unlock (collaboration, i18n)
ai_era_implications: AI velocity makes multi-year freezes harder—modularize so AI ships on branches

intelligence.pattern_name: Ugly baby protection for fragile ideas
intelligence.source_episode: The woman behind Canva shares how she built a $42B company from nothing | Melanie Perkins
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: New ideas are like ugly babies… leadership has to protect them until they can stand on their own.
intelligence.core_belief: Early ideas are socially vulnerable; premature critique kills nonlinear bets.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Separate ideation forums from execution critique; celebrate CBGs even when rungs look embarrassing.
contradictions: Protect ideas vs ship fast culture
emotional_political_dynamics: Shame on small first steps vs huge mission
ai_era_implications: Cheap prototypes increase ugly babies—need faster kill criteria too

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Bob's job is to make you the worst version of yourself... emotions under two weeks are Bob; longer than two weeks, talk to someone.
intelligence.core_belief: Scaling emotions are normal and must not drive operational decisions.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Name the inner critic → don't act on 9 PM impulses → revisit after ~2 weeks → address structural issues only if persistent.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Senior leaders still attacked by Bob despite experience.
hidden_assumptions: That sleep fixes structural misalignment; that fighting reorgs preserves value.
pressure_scenarios: Layering, Legos talks, performance cycles, M&A integration.
failure_modes: Sending rage emails; blocking hires; public undermining of new scope owners.
contradictions: Almost always advises embracing change, rarely endorses fighting it.
behavioral_signals: Passive-aggressive Slack; sudden disengagement after reorg announcement.
emotional_political_dynamics: Shame spiral: 'who gave me this job?'
operational_adaptations: Manager helps focus on future; career-long lens—'careers are long.'
ai_era_implications: Faster change cycles compress Bob waves; two-week heuristic may shorten.

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: 80% of culture is literally defined by the personality of the founder... operators articulate and extend, not reshape.
intelligence.core_belief: Values on walls that contradict founder behavior create dissonance.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Diagnose founder personality → encode decisions/hiring/firing as culture → build company that decides like founder absent.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Facebook = Mark; Google = Larry/Sergey PhD paradise vs dorm-room ship culture.
hidden_assumptions: That values workshops change felt culture; consulting frameworks override founder.
pressure_scenarios: Co-founder power imbalance; operator hired to 'fix culture.'
failure_modes: Cultural initiatives contradict founder ambiguity tolerance; performative values.
contradictions: Stopped doing values exercises after Facebook despite advising culture work.
behavioral_signals: One all-hands sentence moves company more than months of programs.
emotional_political_dynamics: Operators' humiliation realizing limited culture leverage.
operational_adaptations: Focus on systems: how you hire, fire, decide—not slogans.
ai_era_implications: AI agents trained on founder tone may scale culture faster—for better or worse.

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Escalation is a tool—go together up when stuck, not tattling.
intelligence.core_belief: Peer deadlock wastes time; power must be invoked early.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Two equals stuck → joint escalation with shared case → decision → move.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Zuck/CZI and Facebook norm against anti-escalation shame.
pressure_scenarios: Cross-functional PM/engineering stalemates; exec peer conflict.
failure_modes: Week-long debates; political coalition-building instead of escalate.
contradictions: None strong—anti-pattern is culturally dominant elsewhere.
behavioral_signals: Surprise when exec happy you escalated early.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of looking weak; career risk in toxic cultures.
operational_adaptations: Leaders invite escalation explicitly (pairs with Matt MacInnis Rippling norm).
ai_era_implications: Agents won't escalate; humans must retain escalation norms in hybrid teams.

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: As soon as you're comfortable building houses, someone says it's a neighborhood... pass the house off or get buried under the Lego pile.
intelligence.core_belief: Staying on top of the growing scope requires repeatedly giving away what you're best at.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Rehire yourself every few weeks at hypergrowth; expect scope jumps (house → neighborhood → country).
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Facebook-style operator giving away job every ~3 weeks at peak scale.
hidden_assumptions: That expertise in current scope is long-term job security.
pressure_scenarios: New hire layered above you; reorg; promotion freeze while scope doubles.
failure_modes: Clinging to IC craft; sabotaging successor; territorial questions about new hires.
contradictions: Advise embrace change while acknowledging Bob never goes away.
behavioral_signals: 'Why are we hiring that person?'; rage-email impulses at 9 PM.
emotional_political_dynamics: Joy+fear+territoriality cocktail on every scope change.
operational_adaptations: Externalize emotions as 'Bob'; two-week rule—persistent feelings are signal, Bob waves are noise.
ai_era_implications: AI accelerates scope jumps; leaders must give away analytical tasks to agents and reskill upward faster.

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Growing more than 100% headcount/year is a bad idea; 50% happy, 100% manageable, beyond doubling = dedupe chaos.
intelligence.core_belief: More people ≠ faster; duplication tax dominates.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Question every role opening; prefer quality hire over panic hire; expect duplicate roles if doubling.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Post-2021 layoff-aware operators; AI-era 'scale with tools not people' echo.
hidden_assumptions: That revenue growth requires proportional hiring.
pressure_scenarios: 50→150 plan; sales model spits headcount requests.
failure_modes: Two hires same mandate meet; months deduping; politics over turf.
contradictions: AI companies at 200M ARR with ~100 people challenge old rules.
behavioral_signals: Fear of adding people vs fear of missing targets.
operational_adaptations: Slow hiring with rigorous role deduping questions.
ai_era_implications: Headcount discipline plus AI leverage becomes default survival combo.

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Chamath: stairs are boring; jump off cliff, fall ~6-9 months, climb out beyond where stairs could get you.
intelligence.core_belief: Prove-to-yourself jumps beat title increments for capability expansion.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Take deeply unqualified stretch role → endure low ratings/confusion → emerge with transferable craft (e.g., mobile at Facebook).
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Fast-growing company as cliff factory (Facebook mobile phone project).
hidden_assumptions: That all fear is green light; conflating financial fear with capability fear.
pressure_scenarios: Parent/boss/dad say don't jump; performance review cliff.
failure_modes: Permanent fall (true failure); staying on stairs out of title addiction.
contradictions: Celebrate risk while counseling financial runway math.
behavioral_signals: Professional idiot questions in rooms; dumb questions that weren't dumb.
operational_adaptations: Separate existential vs specific financial anxiety; runway/burn-rate exercise before leap.
ai_era_implications: Learning velocity matters more than accumulated credentials; AI lowers cost of 'professional idiot' learning if embraced.

intelligence.pattern_name: Serve the Business Not the People
intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Best decisions ask what's right for the business—people stuff falls away; direct is kind; barnacles drag ship.
intelligence.core_belief: Company success is the shared goal that resolves political knots.
intelligence.operational_pattern: If-no-emotions test for firing/reorg → communicate kindly → invest in high performers don't ignore them.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Glue Club leaders firing as skill; 50% hiring wrong half the time accepted.
hidden_assumptions: Business success automatically benefits all; kindness equals avoidance.
failure_modes: Keeping low performers; optimizing for being liked.
contradictions: Kindness mantra vs serve-business coldness—integrated via direct kindness.
behavioral_signals: Leaders spend all time on strugglers; high performers unvisited.
emotional_political_dynamics: Manager guilt; Bob amplifying harm avoidance.
operational_adaptations: High-performer experiments: bigger projects, less oversight.
ai_era_implications: Workforce reduction debates get clearer with business-first frame—still emotionally brutal.

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: 80% of team problems are structural or dynamics issues, not people... start at goals, roles, expectations.
intelligence.core_belief: People blame is lazy; unclear systems create pseudo-performance problems.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Waterline model: structural (goals/roles) → dynamics → interpersonal → intrapersonal; fix top layers first.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Glue Club operators re-describing the elephant endlessly.
hidden_assumptions: That one conversation clarifies roles; that OKR spreadsheets equal goals.
pressure_scenarios: Rapid hiring; post-IPO confusion; new manager inheritance.
failure_modes: PIP before alignment; firing without role clarity check.
contradictions: Also advocates fast firing when misfit is real after alignment attempts.
behavioral_signals: Employee describes tiger, manager thought elephant; 100-line OKR docs.
emotional_political_dynamics: Managers feel broken record; employees anxious about unknown success criteria.
operational_adaptations: Two-way dialogue: 'here's what I see—what do you think your job is?'
ai_era_implications: AI can't fix ambiguous roles; automation amplifies misaligned incentives.

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: No company needs more than three company goals... strategy should hurt; one goal wins in a fight; one owner per goal.
intelligence.core_belief: Goals are communication tools, not accounting exercises.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Max 3 company goals → explicit priority winner (Facebook: engagement over MAU tricks) → intern-readable metrics → painful non-goals → named owner → ritual follow-up.
hidden_assumptions: That more goals increase alignment; shared ownership works.
pressure_scenarios: Founder can't cut list; board wants 10 priorities; stage mismatch (annual goals at seed).
failure_modes: 100-line OKR spreadsheets; goals set then ignored; co-owned goals = unowned.
contradictions: Bone to pick with OKRs while using OKR-adjacent framing.
behavioral_signals: Teams working on migration while claiming different top priority.
operational_adaptations: Seed: 2-month iterative goals; established: annual with reset discipline.
ai_era_implications: Faster markets require shorter goal cycles; AI planning tools risk fake precision.

intelligence.source_episode: Molly Graham — The high-growth handbook
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: LinkedIn crush vs dread doing job—'what does this get you that you don't already have?'
intelligence.core_belief: Titles and shoulds delay listening to energy and fit.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Long proving career → wall → pivot to community/craft (Glue Club) not next C-level.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Operator famous for founder support rejects fancy COO roles.
hidden_assumptions: Next rung is always up; audience expects continuous climbing.
pressure_scenarios: C-level offers; family programming; peer comparison.
failure_modes: Take role for LinkedIn post; stay years in misaligned work.
contradictions: Advice for climbers while choosing descaler path.
behavioral_signals: Excitement at offer signature, dread at work content.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of disappointing admirers; AC/DC current metaphor.
operational_adaptations: Walks repeating mantra; build safe leader spaces.
ai_era_implications: Faster cycles may compress proving phase—earlier identity crises.

intelligence.pattern_name: AI productivity is not halving headcount
intelligence.source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: HTTP server 50% faster isn't productivity… enables harder work… changed mental model, friction, cognitive load.
intelligence.core_belief: Copilot-class tools shift work from writing to reviewing; workforce cuts from micro-benchmarks are category error.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Study holistic outcomes; communicate balanced metrics to CEOs; prepare SPACE evolution for trust/reliability.
contradictions: Experts love AI vs fear replacement
operational_adaptations: Define problem before buying DevEx tools
ai_era_implications: PMs plan review-heavy SDLC; juniors need mentors not only autocomplete

intelligence.source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Four metrics: lead time, deploy frequency, MTTR, change fail rate… speed and stability move in tandem.
intelligence.core_belief: Fast and stable delivery is achievable with capabilities not slogans.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Measure four keys; benchmark elite; improve capabilities per Accelerate research.
contradictions: Elite targets vs legacy release trains
behavioral_signals: Rising fail rate with deploy push
operational_adaptations: Pair DORA with SPACE for local metrics
ai_era_implications: AI codegen speed is not productivity—review-heavy workflow needs new metrics

intelligence.source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Frictionless is about capabilities and practices that reduce friction—not declaring less process.
intelligence.core_belief: Sustainable speed comes from technical and organizational capabilities documented in Accelerate.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Invest in CI, architecture, psychological safety, and small batches—not only AI licenses.
hidden_assumptions: Not using frictionless as excuse to cut needed process
contradictions: AI replaces process narrative vs capabilities book
ai_era_implications: AI amplifies existing friction if capabilities weak

intelligence.source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Measurement journey: more subjective early, more systemic later—you need both.
intelligence.core_belief: Mature measurement builds from interviews and surveys toward system metrics—not only dashboards day one.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Phase 1: interviews and surveys define problem; Phase 2: instrument systems; Phase 3: correlate and refine.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Org starting DevEx or AI productivity program
pressure_scenarios: Board wants AI ROI number in 30 days
contradictions: Google-scale methods at startup scale
behavioral_signals: Can't answer what problem we're solving
operational_adaptations: Google-style phases adapted to company size
ai_era_implications: AI tool ROI requires phased methodology not single metric

intelligence.source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Never use lines of code—it's the worst metric for knowledge work.
intelligence.core_belief: Activity metrics that reward volume destroy quality and incentives.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Ban LOC/PR count as performance metrics; use outcomes, flow, and satisfaction.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Any engineering org under AI pressure
contradictions: Need some activity signal for capacity planning
behavioral_signals: Review time explodes while LOC up
emotional_political_dynamics: Middle managers default to visible activity
ai_era_implications: AI makes LOC even more misleading

intelligence.source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Write down the problem you're trying to solve in a week—then hunt for signals.
intelligence.core_belief: Tool-first AI productivity programs fail without diagnosed bottleneck.
intelligence.operational_pattern: One-week problem definition workshop before buying copilot/enterprise AI seats.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Enterprise rolling out AI coding tools
contradictions: Move fast adopt AI vs diagnose first
ai_era_implications: AI tool evals should include developer trust not only speed

intelligence.source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: SPACE: Satisfaction, Performance, Activity, Communication, Efficiency/flow—use at least three dimensions.
intelligence.core_belief: Complex creative work requires balanced intentional metrics—not one vanity number.
intelligence.operational_pattern: After DORA diagnostic pick context-fit metrics across SPACE; triangulate surveys with telemetry.
contradictions: DORA fixed four vs SPACE pick-your-own
operational_adaptations: Write problem statement in a week; hunt any signal
ai_era_implications: Add trust/reliance dimension for AI tools

intelligence.pattern_name: Surveys beat instrumentation when they conflict
intelligence.source_episode: How to measure AI developer productivity in 2025 | Nicole Forsgren
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: When surveys disagree with advanced instrumentation, surveys are almost always correct.
intelligence.core_belief: Felt experience captures friction telemetry misses.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Triangulate DevEx surveys with system metrics; act on survey when conflict.
failure_modes: Ignoring survey because data says fine
contradictions: Objective data religion vs qualitative signal
behavioral_signals: Developers cite flow breaks instrumentation misses
operational_adaptations: Week-one problem statement plus any signal hunt
ai_era_implications: Measure review burden and trust in AI output

intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Meta shed ~30k and rehired ~8k AI-first—structural not cyclical.
intelligence.core_belief: Headcount reduction plus recomposition is the new restructuring playbook.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Cut coordination layers; rehire builders with AI fluency; expect smaller but denser teams.
hidden_assumptions: Rehire numbers may not net reduce pain
failure_modes: Rehiring same profiles without behavior change
contradictions: Public layoffs while claiming AI opportunity
behavioral_signals: Smaller teams owning more surface area
ai_era_implications: Default org design is smaller, denser, more automated

intelligence.pattern_name: Build internal tools not status reports
intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: PMs should build the tools that replace the meetings and decks they used to own.
intelligence.core_belief: Automation of PM coordination work is the highest-leverage PM task.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Replace recurring syncs with dashboards/agents; PM maintains the system not the slide.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Product org automating its own coordination tax
contradictions: Anti-meeting while requiring alignment
ai_era_implications: Agents absorb information-moving PM work first

intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Half of PMs are builders; half aren't—and the split will widen.
intelligence.core_belief: The profession splits into builders and coordinators with different career trajectories.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Hire and promote PMs who ship code/prototypes; redeploy or exit coordinators.
hidden_assumptions: Building skill is trainable for some coordinators
Not all domains need PMs who code
failure_modes: Burnout from PMs forced to code
contradictions: Advocates joy/adoption while pushing harder building bar
operational_adaptations: Pair PMs with eng on tools
ai_era_implications: Builder PM becomes minimum viable PM

intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: If your job is moving information between people, AI will do that; PMs who don't build will be in trouble.
intelligence.core_belief: Coordination-only PM work is automatable; building is the new baseline.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Shift PM work from status aggregation to building prototypes, tools, and decisions; measure output not meetings.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Large tech org shedding non-builder PM layers
hidden_assumptions: Not all PM work is information movement
failure_modes: PMs resist building and become expendable
contradictions: Still values influence skills while condemning information-moving
ai_era_implications: PM headcount shrinks; builder-PMs absorb eng-adjacent work

intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: People adopt tools that feel joyful, not just productive.
intelligence.core_belief: Emotional pull beats mandate for tool uptake.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Design rollouts for delight first; measure smiles and voluntary usage before compliance metrics.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Enterprise rolling out AI productivity stack
hidden_assumptions: Joy without security creates shadow IT
Mandates still needed for regulated workflows
contradictions: Joy framing vs forced enterprise rollout
emotional_political_dynamics: Resistance from burned prior tool rollouts
ai_era_implications: Joy becomes primary enterprise adoption KPI

intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Middle experience PMs face highest risk—not cheap enough, not visionary enough.
intelligence.core_belief: The barbell hollows the middle of PM experience distribution.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Senior PMs must show builder+strategy; juniors must show AI-native speed.
contradictions: Advice to reinvent while cutting mid-level roles
operational_adaptations: Move up to strategy or down to building
ai_era_implications: Middle PM layer thins fastest

intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: AI-first is not optional at this point—debating it is wasted calories.
intelligence.core_belief: Organizations must assume AI-first operating model as baseline.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Rewrite workflows assuming AI participation; stop pilot-only mentality.
hidden_assumptions: Some workflows still need human-only paths
contradictions: Mandate vs thoughtful safety pacing
ai_era_implications: AI-first becomes table stakes like mobile-first

intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: If you haven't reinvented yourself in last 2-3 years, you're at risk.
intelligence.core_belief: Career capital decays quickly in AI transition.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Force periodic skill resets; treat past employer brand as liability not asset.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Individual knowledge worker in platform shift
contradictions: Critiques resume brands while using Meta/Google pedigree
ai_era_implications: Reinvention cycle compresses from years to months

intelligence.source_episode: Why half of product managers are in trouble | Nikhyl Singhal (Meta, Google)
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Hiring on resume brand selects for coasting; undiscovered talent has alpha.
intelligence.core_belief: Prestige credentials anti-correlate with hunger in current market.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Source from non-obvious networks; test with real work samples; ignore logo resumes.
hidden_assumptions: Brand still correlates with baseline skill
contradictions: Anti-brand stance from ex-Meta/Google executive
ai_era_implications: AI work samples replace resume screening

intelligence.source_episode: Rachel Lockett — Difficult conversations
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Leaders over-rotate advising—training teams to bring every hard problem; coaching unlocks brilliance if person has context to solve.
intelligence.core_belief: Expert hires don't need you to own their domain problems.
intelligence.operational_pattern: 15% more open questions in 1:1s → GROW model → advise only when urgent or skill gap.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Stripe/Pinterest HRBP → exec coach for AI founders.
hidden_assumptions: Coaching is hour-long sessions; leaders fear looking non-expert.
pressure_scenarios: CEO bottleneck on every decision; talent review disagreements.
failure_modes: Guess-my-solution games; coaching when they need exact playbook (IPO steps).
contradictions: Coach stance but strong talent opinions in exec forums.
behavioral_signals: Team asks permission not forgiveness; founder resentment at being hub.
emotional_political_dynamics: Ego of leader as fixer; shame when strong negative reaction.
operational_adaptations: Active listening levels 1-3; label feelings; Jeff case study squads+KPIs.
ai_era_implications: Leaders may offload coaching to AI—risk if empathy/signal reading lost.

intelligence.source_episode: Rachel Lockett — Difficult conversations
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Stripe top-50 exhausted; aim 80% time in gifts—name interests to allies, hire around weaknesses (visionary CEO + COO).
intelligence.core_belief: Burnout is design failure, not badge of honor.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Future-self test: will I resent this choice? → prioritize sleep/health/kids → no balance, intentional prioritization.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Founder job morph every 6 months post-fundraise.
hidden_assumptions: 80% achievable in executive roles; company will accommodate gift focus.
pressure_scenarios: Board management after product shipped; fundraising identity shift.
failure_modes: Three years on wrong job post-PMF; acqui-hire identity crisis.
contradictions: Ambitious 80% goal in roles with infinite demands.
behavioral_signals: Dread Sunday; spouse criticism; excitement loss.
emotional_political_dynamics: Shame leaving 'successful' track; LinkedIn crush vs job dread.
operational_adaptations: Contract with manager on growth path; COO hire for internal ops hatred.
ai_era_implications: AI may compress grunt work enabling gifts—or accelerate burnout if boundaries weak.

intelligence.source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Not AI as a replacement for people, but AI as a way to amplify people.
intelligence.core_belief: Human expertise stays in the loop; AI multiplies judgment throughput.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Participant text→AI draft feedback→coach edit; style-tuned prompts.
contradictions: Accelerated AI roadmap after delay plan
ai_era_implications: Enterprise template: expert amplification with structured artifacts

intelligence.source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: At a startup, you can't do that [experimental approach]... shift to much more of a conviction-oriented approach.
intelligence.core_belief: Pre-scale decisions are conviction bets tested by speed.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Set informed-conviction threshold; ship; pivot on qualitative signal.
contradictions: Rigorous strategy vs anti-early-experiment
ai_era_implications: Pre-PMF AI needs trace conviction loops not benchmarks

intelligence.source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: If you're building APIs, then your experience is actually the API spec.
intelligence.core_belief: PM craft defines experience artifacts, not feature lists.
intelligence.operational_pattern: 12-competency model: UX design = experience for APIs, models, or UI.
contradictions: Episode title: JSON before design for AI prototypes
ai_era_implications: AI PMs own prompt/schema/eval as primary UX

intelligence.source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: If you don't understand how to move a particular metric, then the right goal is to set a goal to increase your understanding.
intelligence.core_belief: Outcome obsession ≠ committing to unmoved metrics.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Classify goals as understanding, dependency, execution, or strategic risk; use hit×know-why 2×2.
contradictions: NCTs vs OKR orthodoxy
ai_era_implications: Agent teams need failure-taxonomy goals before resolution-rate targets

intelligence.source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: The company mission, the company strategy, the product strategy, the product roadmap, and the product goals all work together as a system.
intelligence.core_belief: Prioritization fights mean missing strategy.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Debug bottom-up; define top-down; use Tinder vs Hinge to decide copy vs diverge.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: VP Product with PMs stuck between equal options
hidden_assumptions: Vision must be separate from mission
contradictions: Goals after roadmap but strategy before goals
ai_era_implications: AI bets need capability strategy before autonomy metrics

intelligence.source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: If you don't feel confident in the direction that your team is moving, the right answer is to micromanage, but do it in a very tactical and a very temporary way.
intelligence.core_belief: Hands-off leadership when direction is wrong is worse than temporary tactical control.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Map confidence×autonomy; use scalable leadership when aligned; selective micromanagement until frameworks transfer decision rights.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Product leadership coach for CPOs with builder instincts
hidden_assumptions: Senior leaders should only do strategy
contradictions: Praises famous micromanagers while coaching against the label
operational_adaptations: Exit when team exceeds leader on decisions
ai_era_implications: AI leaders must intervene when teams ship autonomy theater without eval discipline

intelligence.source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: The advantage a smaller company has really is in latency... from an idea to actually being able to test that idea.
intelligence.core_belief: Speed is cycle time to learning, not output volume.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Shrink work batches; ship testable slices daily; measure idea-to-result latency.
intelligence.organizational_archetype: Founder after big-co career (Outpace, ex-Tinder CPO)
contradictions: Contradicts big-co efficiency narrative
behavioral_signals: Founders say startup feels slower at first
operational_adaptations: Conviction decisions when N too small
ai_era_implications: AI startups win on trace/eval iteration speed

intelligence.source_episode: The secret to better AI prototypes: Why Tinder's CPO starts with JSON, not design | Ravi Mehta
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: The strategy doc wouldn't be complete without wireframes... everyone takes away a different interpretation when you talk about strategy in words alone.
intelligence.core_belief: Strategy must bind pixel-level tradeoffs or it is not strategy.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Require low-fi wireframes with strategy reviews; nav-bar litmus for mobile.
contradictions: PMs should sketch—not only designers
ai_era_implications: AI prototypes start with schemas/specs before UI polish

intelligence.source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Close Friends failed: favorites mistranslated, one person added, loop not closed… 2–3 years to find where it worked.

contradictions: Speed culture vs multi-year bake
ai_era_implications: AI features need same loop analytics

intelligence.source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: Competing Against Luck… understand the job to be done not just the feature request.
intelligence.core_belief: Feature failures are often wrong job hypotheses not execution failures.
intelligence.operational_pattern: When feature underperforms, run JTBD analysis before killing or scaling.
contradictions: Data-heavy Google vs qualitative JTBD
operational_adaptations: Close Friends playbook: segment then simplify
ai_era_implications: AI features need job hypothesis not prompt hypothesis

intelligence.source_episode: Inside Google's AI turnaround: The rise of AI Mode, strategy behind AI Overviews, and their vision for AI-powered search | Robby Stein
intelligence.source_quote_or_context: We have distribution and data graphs startups don't… that compounds over time.
intelligence.core_belief: In platform shifts, distribution plus grounding data often beats feature-first startups long-term.
intelligence.operational_pattern: Integrate AI into existing high-intent surfaces; leverage Shopping/Maps/Lens graphs.
contradictions: Innovator's dilemma on ad model
behavioral_signals: AI features in core SERP not only labs
operational_adaptations: Ship in core product not only beta site
